# Virtual Reality in eLearning. Top 10 Use Cases

The eLearning industry continues its rapid growth, becoming increasingly easily accessible. Due to this, the application of virtual reality in eLearning has become one of the most interesting and promising approaches MOOC providers and instructional designers should consider.

13611

13/Jul/2025

**Est. reading time:** 11 minutes

Virtual reality in eLeraning offers an immersive, distraction-free learning environment that boosts retention by up to 75%, according to PwC. For example, UPS uses VR to simulate dangerous driving conditions in a safe, repeatable format. Compared to traditional methods, trainees learn 4x faster with greater confidence.

One of the main challenges of online education is keeping the learner engaged, as both the attention spans and efficient effort concentration time keep decreasing. The learners nowadays should be engaged within the first 8 seconds of the session or they lose focus and can concentrate on the task for 15 minutes max before they are distracted. This is why various instructional design techniques for learner engagement are widespread.

However, despite making the emphasis on video, infographics, tests and quizzes, [gamification](https://raccoongang.com/blog/designing-efficient-elearning-gamification/), social network integration,  [learning management systems](https://raccoongang.com/blog/what-lms-learn-basics/) nowadays can provide so much immersion only. The learner is still in front of the screen and cannot interact with the instructor amidst the video.

VR offers a whole other level of immersion, where the learner can interact with items and characters in the virtual surroundings and gets a much more engaging experience in comparison with online courses or brick-and-mortar education.

Here are just some examples of virtual reality in education, entertainment, and training:

1. [Google offers their VR headsets to schools for free](https://www.theverge.com/2015/9/28/9409571/google-expeditions-virtual-reality-field-trips) along with educational field trips.
2. [Microsoft immerses the players into Minecraft metaverse using Rift virtual reality](https://arstechnica.com/gaming/2016/08/minecraft-vr-mod-shuts-down-as-microsoft-adds-official-rift-support/). This also helps the children express their creativity through building and exploring in the virtual world.
3. [Conquer Mobile tests surgical training simulations to help medics](https://twitter.com/despinachrisos/status/705631023514529792).
4. [The largest bank of Australia allows applicants explore their potential working environment through VR](https://www.linkedin.com/business/talent/blog/talent-acquisition/australias-biggest-bank-uses-virtual-reality-to-recruit).
5. [CITEC lets you have our own VR gym. Personal trainer included](https://phys.org/news/2016-02-smart-physical-virtual-reality.html).
6. [NASA uses VR for preparing astronauts for upcoming missions](https://www.digitaltrends.com/virtual-reality/how-nasa-is-using-virtual-reality-to-train-astronauts/).

   Photo credit: NASA
7. [Toyota teaches the teens to drive with their TeenDrive365 campaign](https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2015/jan/15/toyota-oculus-rift-virtual-reality-distracted-driving) to deliver immersive driving experiences without endangering the drivers, vehicles and pedestrians.
8. [The US Military uses VR to train soldiers](https://www.livescience.com/10022-military-video-games.html), which also helps minimize training expenses.

   Photo credit: US military
9. [Siemens](https://www.siemens.com/global/en.html) and other companies from oil and gas, chemical, metallurgy and other high-risk industries also benefit from providing such employee training simulations. When the outcomes of the decisions can be simulated in virtual reality, the mistakes will not lead to damage and casualties, greatly increasing the efficiency of the training.

Thus said, VR will undoubtedly find even more ways into the eLearning industry, yet it has both pros and cons and we will list them below.

## Benefits of VR in eLearning

1. **Immersion into the learning.** VR set blocks distractions like social networks, phones, TV ads and other informational noise, allowing the learners to concentrate on the learning. Submersion into an interesting 3D simulation with lots of potent stimuli leads to naturally improving the brain’s ability to process new information, granting an unprecedented level of knowledge comprehension.
2. **Personalized [learning environment](https://raccoongang.com/blog/what-is-a-virtual-learning-environment/)s.** Designers can create any environment to provide comfort for the learners. Every learner can choose the surrounding that fits them best — a forest, a seashore, a sunny lawn, and starry skies at night. This will help the learners forget they sit in a classroom and relax to comprehend new knowledge better.
3. **Training both knowledge and physical skills.** Serious gaming and simulations can reach quite another level, where a worker must execute the correct sequence of movements (open the toolbox, take the right tool, shut down the valve, cut the needed wire, pull the correct switch, etc.) Such exercises will create muscle memory, helping the employees be prepared to dangerous situations without incurring great expenses on the company and cutting down the risk in a dangerous simulation.

Even though virtual reality is in its infancy nowadays, it already has much to offer in terms of eLearning. As time will pass, more and more applications will reveal themselves.

## Factors slowing down the implementation of VR in Online Learning

1. **Financial restraints**. VR set nowadays is a sizeable investment, limiting its usage to a narrow circle of tech geeks and early adopters, treating it more as a toy than a tool for education. Smartphones were also not too widespread 10 years ago and now almost 80% of the US or EU population have them. We are sure that with time VR sets will also become more affordable and turn into ubiquitous parts of our lives.
2. **Hardware requirements**. As of now, VR set requires quite an expensive computer to process the immersive simulations. Add the high-speed unlimited Internet connection (for delivering eLearning) into the equation and you end up with significant hardware requirements. As hardware development will progress, computing power level needed for delivering [immersive learning](https://raccoongang.com/blog/immersive-learning-explained/) simulations will become lower, leading to wider adoption of VR in eLearning.
3. **Physical limitations**. When people see VR goggles, they rarely think of all the accessories like manipulators that actually form the VR set needed to provide full immersion into virtual reality. This system limits the freedom to move as it needs to be stationary. mLearning is on the rise because people want to learn whenever and wherever they want, a feature VR cannot yet provide. Once VR sets become more compact and ergonomic, their adoption potential should grow exponentially.
4. **Physiological issues**.  Unfortunately, nausea is quite probable when using a VR set due to the issues with screen latency. The manufacturers are doing their best to overcome the challenge, and it is only a question of time. However, the issue still persists and limits the use of virtual reality in eLearning greatly. Such distractions are not helping in learning.

## Top 10 Real-World VR eLearning Examples

Virtual reality (VR) technology has seen remarkable advancements in recent years, unlocking transformative applications across diverse industries. In this article, we will explore some of the most compelling uses of VR by pioneering platforms and organizations, such as Discovery VR, Labster, VirtualSpeech, Google Earth VR, InMind 2, Sketchbox, Boulevard, and SkyView. These examples showcase how VR is revolutionizing education, healthcare, corporate training, and creative fields with immersive and innovative solutions.

**1. VR Field Trips by Discovery.** Virtual field trips using platforms like [Discovery VR in education](https://www.discoveryeducation.com/community/virtual-field-trips/) and Google Expeditions give students a realistic way to explore global landmarks, natural wonders, and historical sites without leaving the classroom. These experiences create engaging learning opportunities, making subjects like history and geography come alive through 3D and 360-degree video content. With over 1,000 destinations available for virtual reality learning, students can “travel” to locations such as the Eiffel Tower, the Amazon Rainforest, or Ancient Egypt. This example of virtual reality in education helps in broadening their cultural horizons, contextual understanding, and increasing knowledge retention by up to 75%​.

**2. Medical and Nursing Training.** VR simulations for medical training, offered by platforms like [Labster](https://www.labster.com/) and [UbiSim](https://www.ubisimvr.com/), replicate real-life medical situations, including patient interactions, surgical procedures, and anatomy exploration. This approach allows students to practice high-risk tasks in a safe and controlled environment, leading to 63% higher knowledge retention rates and reducing training costs by up to 50%. Virtual scenarios such as emergency response and diagnostic evaluations provide hands-on examples of virtual reality in education that are otherwise hard to achieve in traditional training.

**3. STEM and Virtual Labs.** Platforms like Labster provide interactive VR labs where students conduct virtual experiments across disciplines like chemistry, physics, and engineering. These labs offer features such as molecular visualization and real-time data analysis, allowing students to conduct tasks like gene editing or chemical synthesis that would be impossible in a regular [classroom](https://raccoongang.com/blog/what-virtual-classroom/). The gamified approach makes VR in eLearning more engaging, helping to improve STEM test scores by 76% and democratizing access to high-quality scientific education for all students.

**5. Historical Simulations.** By using VR simulations such as the [1943 Berlin Blitz](https://www.bbc.com/historyofthebbc/100-voices/ww2/360berlin), students gain an immersive understanding of historical events. The combination of authentic footage, sound effects, and narration allows learners to experience history from a first-person perspective, resulting in a 50% higher retention rate for details about historical events compared to conventional learning methods. These experiences help students build a stronger emotional and cognitive connection to the past​.

Soft Skills Development with VirtualSpeech.** [VirtualSpeech](https://virtualspeech.com/) uses VR to simulate real-life scenarios such as public speaking or customer service interactions, providing realistic practice environments for developing communication skills. Learners report a 275% increase in confidence after using VR for soft skills training, as the simulations help them practice in diverse situations without the pressure of live audiences. **7. Exploring the Human Brain with [InMind 2](https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/inmind-2-vr-cardboard/id1166999366).** It offers an interactive, gamified approach to learning about neuroscience. Students navigate through a virtual brain to understand the functions of neurotransmitters and neural pathways. ## The Bottom Line

As you can see, the use of VR in eLearning promises some great results and faces some dire challenges. However, the most important question is this: will you be ready to [implement VR into your eLearning practices](https://raccoongang.com/open-edx-services/custom-feature-development/) once its issues are dealt with? Sharp-minded entrepreneurs should have ready methodologies in place to reap the benefits once the technology is ready for mass adoption.

VR in Higher Education: with Examples
-------------------------------------

October 18, 2024 - Verity Healy

Virtual Reality (VR) has the potential to transform the way we learn and teach, from providing in-depth knowledge and helping us understand complex subjects to facilitating language immersion and virtual trips.

It’s already widely used in[schools for younger children](https://virtualspeech.com/blog/vr-educational-experiences-classroom), but we’re seeing its use more and more in higher education for a variety of reasons, including its ability to enhance learning and help students build their real-world skills and employability.

In this article, we make the case for using VR in higher education, illustrating how and why it’s being used, with real-life examples of universities, business schools, and colleges that are already taking full advantage of the technology.

### Benefits of Immersive Learning Tools

Immersive learning tools like Virtual Reality (VR) are transforming education by creating dynamic, interactive environments where students can engage with content in ways traditional methods cannot offer. These tools open new possibilities for various aspects of learning, from remote education to skill development.

#### Enhancing Learning through Immersive Experiences

One of the primary benefits of VR in education is its ability to transform traditional lectures into [immersive, hands on experiences](https://virtualspeech.com/blog/experiential-learning-vr), bringing abstract concepts and complex subjects to life. By allowing students to engage with 3D simulations and virtual environments, VR enhances interactivity and boosts engagement, making it easier for students to grasp and retain information.

![Image 6: ASU taking part in the Dreamscape Learning Lab Biology](https://vs-static.virtualspeech.com/img/blog/asu-dreamscape-learning-lab-biology.jpg)

_Students at ASU taking part in the Dreamscape Learning Lab Biology experience._

The practical applications of VR span across disciplines. Civil engineering students can design and visualize buildings in 3D environments, while history students can virtually [navigate Roman ruins](https://unimersiv.com/ancient-rome-virtual-reality/) or other historical sites, deepening their understanding through exploration. Physics students can use VR to [explore the universe](https://www.overviewexperience.com/), making abstract concepts more tangible. These immersive experiences make learning more impactful and accessible.

For example, Arizona State University (ASU)recently partnered with Dreamscape to launch the Dreamscape [Learn Lab](https://news.asu.edu/20221021-creativity-vr-biology-lab-experience-leads-student-success), where students learn biology through VR-enhanced experiences incorporating hand tracking and haptic feedback. Data from the program showed that students participating in the VR labs had significantly higher engagement and improved lab grades compared to their peers in traditional lab courses.

_The[Esade](https://www.esade.edu/)library is rolling out a new Virtual Reality content service for students, faculty, and staff with the[VirtualSpeech platform](https://virtualspeech.com/education)._

Beyond STEM subjects, VR is making an impact in other fields as well. ESADE business school has implemented VR content for students, faculty, and staff to practice key skills and build employability through immersive, repeatable experiences. This approach allows for consistent, realistic practice, making it easier to develop soft skills and prepare for real-world challenges.

#### VR for Distance Learning

As global education expands, VR’s ability to support distance learning is becoming increasingly important. VR removes geographical barriers, allowing students from anywhere in the world to attend classes, participate in discussions, and collaborate with peers in real-time, as though they were physically present.

Leading institutions have already embraced this technology. For example, Stanford School of Business offered a [certificate program delivered entirely through VR](https://www.gsb.stanford.edu/experience/news-history/virtual-reality-online-lead-program-exceeded-expectations) as early as 2016, and in 2022, medical students from Queen Mary University of London experienced their [first lectures in “the metaverse”](https://www.qmul.ac.uk/media/news/2022/pr/queen-mary-students-receive-first-lecture-in-the-metaverse.html).

![Image 7: iLRN's virtual campus](https://vs-static.virtualspeech.com/img/blog/ilrn-virtual-campus.jpg)
_A lecture at iLRN’s virtual campus hosted on the Virbella platform._

Social VR platforms like AltSpace and VR Chat, along with tailored applications, create virtual spaces where students and lecturers can meet, communicate, and collaborate. These tools provide a highly interactive and accessible experience for remote learners, making distance education more engaging and effective.

Further advancing this concept, the [Immersive Learning Research Network (iLRN)](https://www.immersivelrn.org/initiatives/virtual-campus/) developed a year-round Virtual Campus that supports lectures, events, networking, and their annual conference. This ongoing virtual presence offers an innovative model for continuous learning and global collaboration.

#### VR for Collaboration and Teamwork

Another key benefit of VR is its capacity to enhance collaboration among students. VR platforms enable learners to engage in group projects and brainstorming sessions within shared virtual environments, no matter their physical location. This not only promotes teamwork but also exposes students to new perspectives and problem-solving approaches in ways that traditional methods can’t match.

One notable example is a collaboration between Harvard University and Zhejiang University in China, where students used [rumii](https://store.steampowered.com/app/872440/rumii/), a social VR software, to conduct an anthropological lesson. Half of the students were at Harvard, while the other half were at Zhejiang, working together in a VR-equipped classroom as avatars to study ancient Egyptian characters found in a tomb.

![Image 8: Students in China participating in the Giza Project with Harvard University](https://vs-static.virtualspeech.com/img/blog/giza_project_harvard_university.jpg)

_Students in China participating in the [Giza Project](http://giza.fas.harvard.edu/), with Harvard University ([image source](https://www.dogheadsimulations.com/doghead-blog/2019/1/7/rumii-customer-spotlight-how-harvard-university-uses-virtual-reality-to-explore-the-pyramids))_

Using standalone Oculus Go headsets, they collaborated to identify specific hieroglyphics. Through the use of avatars with mapped facial expressions, students on opposite sides of the world were able to engage in real-time discussions, synthesize information, and learn together, despite the physical distance.

These collaborative spaces foster a sense of belonging and reduce the isolation often experienced in remote learning environments. Whether students are working on joint business presentations or creative design projects, VR offers a platform where they can interact and communicate seamlessly in real time.

### Enhancing University Success through Immersive Solutions

#### Student Recruitment

Recruiting students is highly competitive, and campus tours play a crucial role in the decision-making process. However, visiting multiple universities can be both costly and time-consuming for prospective students and their families. Virtual Reality (VR) tours offer a solution by allowing students to explore campuses from the comfort of their homes, enabling them to narrow down their options more efficiently. For some, a virtual tour may even provide enough information to make their final decision without needing to visit in person.

![Image 9: Inside YouVisit virtual tour of Harvard](https://vs-static.virtualspeech.com/img/blog/youvisit_tour_harvard.jpg)
_Inside YouVisit’s virtual tour of Harvard_.

Companies like [EAB](https://eab.com/products/virtual-tours/) have already created VR tours for prestigious institutions, including Harvard, Princeton, Yale, and Columbia. On average, these virtual tours engage users for 10.4 minutes, result in an 18% increase in inquiries, and lead to a 27% increase in physical visits to the universities after the virtual experience. These numbers demonstrate the impact of virtual tours on student recruitment, offering a cost-effective way to attract prospective students while enhancing their decision-making process.

#### Maximizing ROI on Training Costs

Universities and colleges must continually invest in modern equipment, such as lab apparatus, medical devices, and chemistry sets, to provide hands-on learning experiences for students. However, budget constraints often mean students have limited access to this equipment, sharing it with others and reducing valuable practice time.

VR training simulators provide an innovative solution by allowing institutions to scale training programs at a much lower cost. This not only increases access but also enhances learning opportunities, making VR a valuable tool for maximizing return on investment in educational resources.

=================================================

The way children learn is undergoing a revolutionary transformation, with immersive learning taking center stage. No longer confined to textbooks and traditional classroom instruction, interactive learning experiences are helping kids absorb knowledge in ways that are more engaging, effective, and enjoyable. By integrating cutting-edge technologies such as virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), gamification, and AI-driven learning, educators and parents are creating dynamic environments where children can explore, experiment, and interact with concepts in ways that were never before possible.

Immersive learning is more than just an exciting trend—it is proven to enhance comprehension, critical thinking, and retention. Research suggests that children who engage in interactive, experiential learning are far more likely to understand complex subjects and apply their knowledge in real-world situations. Whether it’s exploring the solar system in VR, conducting virtual science experiments, or coding interactive games, immersive learning is shaping the future of education by making learning more accessible, engaging, and fun.

We highlighted 10 of the best immersive learning experiences for kids, showcasing how innovative technology is transforming education and opening new doors for young learners. Whether at home, in school, or through [specialized STEM programs](https://waterloo.youngengineers.org/enrichment-programs/), these immersive experiences provide a window into the future of learning, making education more interactive, personalized, and impactful than ever before.

Key Insights:
-------------

*   **Increases engagement**: Interactive learning keeps children actively involved.
*   **Enhances knowledge retention**: Hands-on experiences improve recall and application.
*   **Encourages problem-solving skills**: Kids explore, experiment, and create solutions.
*   **Caters to different learning styles**: Visual, auditory, and kinesthetic learners benefit.
*   **Prepares kids for the future**: Exposure to technology and critical thinking boosts career readiness.

What is Immersive Learning?
---------------------------

Immersive learning is an innovative educational approach that engages students in interactive, multisensory experiences using advanced technology. It moves beyond traditional classroom methods by integrating virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), gamification, artificial intelligence (AI), mixed reality (MR), and interactive simulations to make learning more engaging, effective, and memorable.

Unlike conventional learning methods that rely heavily on textbooks and lectures, [immersive learning provides hands-on, experiential education](https://waterloo.youngengineers.org/workshops/) where children can actively explore and interact with their subjects. By immersing students in real-world scenarios, 3D simulations, and digital environments, this approach fosters deeper understanding, problem-solving skills, and creativity.

### How Immersive Learning Works:

*   **Virtual Reality (VR):** Creates a fully digital environment where students can explore historical sites, conduct virtual science experiments, or [practice STEM concepts in a simulated setting](https://waterloo.youngengineers.org/blog/top-stem-summer-camps-in-canada/).
*   **Augmented Reality (AR):** Overlays digital information onto the real world, enhancing traditional learning materials with 3D animations, interactive models, and live demonstrations.
*   **Gamification:** Incorporates game elements such as challenges, rewards, and leaderboards to make learning fun and competitive.
*   **AI-Powered Learning:** Adapts lessons to each student’s pace, providing personalized learning paths and real-time feedback.
*   **Mixed Reality (MR):** Merges real and virtual environments to create interactive learning experiences, such as holographic science experiments.

### Key Benefits of Immersive Learning:

*   **Enhanced Engagement:** Students remain focused and motivated through interactive and gamified elements.
*   **Better Retention and Understanding:** Hands-on experiences improve knowledge retention and concept comprehension.
*   **Encourages Critical Thinking and Creativity:** Encourages problem-solving by placing students in real-world challenges and decision-making scenarios.
*   **Multisensory Learning:** Combines visual, auditory, and kinesthetic elements to cater to different learning styles.
*   **Safe Learning Environment:** Allows students to practice skills and conduct experiments in a risk-free virtual setting before applying them in real life.

As education continues to evolve, immersive learning is set to play a pivotal role in the future of STEM education, offering children more dynamic, interactive, and impactful ways to learn, explore, and innovate.

Immersive learning is an [interactive educational approach](https://elmlearning.com/blog/immersive-v-interactive-learning/) that leverages cutting-edge technologies to create engaging, hands-on learning experiences. Unlike traditional classroom methods that rely on passive instruction, immersive learning uses virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), gamification, artificial intelligence (AI), and interactive simulations to make education more dynamic and impactful.

This type of learning allows children to actively participate in their education by exploring virtual environments, manipulating 3D objects, solving real-world problems, and engaging in role-playing scenarios. By blending the digital and physical worlds, [immersive learning enhances cognitive development](https://waterloo.youngengineers.org/blog/best-summer-camps-in-waterloo/), creativity, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.

### Key Features of Immersive Learning:

*   **Real-World Simulation**: Enables students to practice skills in a **risk-free, interactive environment**.
*   **Personalized Learning**: AI-powered platforms adapt lessons to **individual learning speeds and styles**.
*   **Increased Engagement**: Interactive elements **keep students motivated** by making lessons feel like an adventure.
*   **Enhanced Retention**: Hands-on learning experiences **improve knowledge retention and recall**.
*   **Multisensory Learning**: Combines **visual, auditory, and kinesthetic** learning for a **well-rounded educational experience**.

As technology continues to evolve, immersive learning is becoming an essential tool in modern education, equipping children with the skills and knowledge needed to thrive in a rapidly changing world.

10 Examples of Immersive Learning for Kids [2025]
-------------------------------------------------

As technology continues to reshape education, immersive learning has become one of the most effective ways to engage children in meaningful experiences. Unlike traditional classroom methods, [immersive learning leverages virtual reality](https://www.gettingsmart.com/2024/06/18/virtual-voyages-augment-and-virtual-reality-point-toward-an-immersive-learning-future/) (VR), augmented reality (AR), gamification, and AI-driven interactivity to bring lessons to life. These methods allow children to interact with content, visualize complex concepts, and participate in hands-on activities that enhance engagement, retention, and critical thinking.

For parents considering a [STEM summer camp for their child](https://bookwhen.com/youngengineerswaterloo), immersive learning experiences offer a unique opportunity to spark curiosity and develop essential skills. Whether through virtual field trips, coding and robotics programs, or hands-on science experiments, immersive learning fosters creativity and problem-solving abilities that extend far beyond the classroom.

Here are 10 of the best immersive learning experiences for kids, designed to make education more dynamic, interactive, and fun.

### 1. Virtual Reality (VR) Field Trips

Virtual reality allows children to visit **historical landmarks, explore space, and dive into the ocean’s depths** without ever leaving their learning environment. Platforms like **Google Expeditions, Discovery VR, and ClassVR** offer fully immersive experiences that transform geography, history, and science lessons into unforgettable adventures.

**How it benefits learning:**

*   Enhances engagement and memory retention by making lessons interactive.
*   Encourages curiosity about history, geography, and science.
*   Studies show that students using VR field trips demonstrate a **50% increase in engagement and recall** compared to traditional learning methods.

### 2. Augmented Reality (AR) Storytelling

Augmented reality adds an **interactive layer to traditional books**, turning reading into an exciting and visually engaging experience. Apps like **Wonderscope, AR Flashcards, and Quiver** use **3D animations, voice narration, and real-time interactions** to immerse children in their favorite stories.

**How it benefits learning:**

*   Improves **reading comprehension, vocabulary, and engagement**.
*   Helps **visual learners** connect with stories in a more meaningful way.
*   Makes literature exciting for children who struggle with traditional reading methods.

### 3. Immersive STEM Learning with Hands-On Experiments

Science and engineering come to life through **interactive tools like LEGO Education kits, Merge Cube, and Microsoft HoloLens**. These platforms allow children to **visualize scientific concepts in 3D, conduct virtual experiments, and develop problem-solving skills** through hands-on learning.

**Real-world impact:**

*   Encourages **critical thinking, logical reasoning, and creativity**.
*   Helps children **understand abstract science and engineering concepts**.
*   Builds confidence in **STEM-related subjects**, preparing kids for future careers.

### 4. Gamified Learning Platforms

Gamification transforms **traditional education into an engaging challenge** where kids earn points, complete missions, and unlock rewards while mastering new subjects. Platforms like **Kahoot!, Prodigy Math, and Duolingo for Kids** make learning fun and competitive.

**Benefits:**

*   Increases **motivation and engagement**, especially in difficult subjects.
*   Improves **problem-solving and critical-thinking skills**.
*   Encourages **self-paced learning**, helping kids progress at their own speed.

### 5. Coding & Robotics Programs

Coding is becoming an essential skill for the future, and programs like **Scratch, LEGO Mindstorms, and Code.org** offer kids the chance to learn programming through hands-on challenges.

**How it benefits learning:**

*   Develops **computational thinking, problem-solving, and logic skills**.
*   Encourages creativity through **interactive coding and robotics projects**.
*   Prepares kids for **STEM careers in AI, software development, and engineering**.

### 6. AI-Powered Personalized Learning Platforms

AI-driven learning tools like **Squirrel AI, DreamBox, and Carnegie Learning** tailor lesson plans to match a child’s skill level, ensuring a **personalized learning experience** that adapts to their strengths and weaknesses.

**How it benefits learning:**

*   Provides **instant feedback** and real-time progress tracking.
*   Adapts to different **learning speeds and abilities**.
*   Helps students **focus on areas needing improvement**, improving overall performance.

### 7. Music and Arts with Virtual Instruments & AR Drawing Apps

Virtual instruments and **AR-powered art applications** allow kids to explore their creative side without expensive materials. Platforms like **Chrome Music Lab, SketchAR, and Just a Line** help children experiment with music composition and digital painting.

**Real-world impact:**

*   Encourages **self-expression and artistic creativity**.
*   Makes music and art **more accessible to children of all backgrounds**.
*   Enhances **fine motor skills and coordination**.

### 8. Role-Playing & Social-Emotional Learning with VR

Virtual reality isn’t just for entertainment—it can also help children **develop social skills, empathy, and communication abilities**. Platforms like **Talespin VR and ImmerseMe** provide simulations that **teach public speaking, teamwork, and emotional intelligence**.

**How it benefits learning:**

*   Helps children **understand different perspectives and emotions**.
*   Builds confidence in **public speaking and group discussions**.
*   Enhances teamwork, leadership, and collaboration skills.

### 9. Hands-On Learning in Virtual Science Labs

For kids who love science, **platforms like Labster and PhET Interactive Simulations** provide virtual labs where they can **conduct experiments in physics, chemistry, and biology**.

**How it benefits learning:**

*   Allows children to **safely explore scientific concepts** in a digital environment.
*   Encourages **curiosity and experimentation**.
*   Improves understanding of **STEM concepts through realistic simulations**.

### 10. Interactive History Simulations

History lessons become **interactive and engaging** with platforms like **TimeLooper and BBC Civilizations AR**, which allow kids to step back in time and experience **historical events in 3D**.

**Benefits:**

*   Brings history to life with **realistic visuals and interactive elements**.
*   Helps students **develop a deeper appreciation for different time periods and cultures**.
*   Makes **historical events more memorable and engaging**..

The Future of Immersive Learning for Kids
-----------------------------------------

The future of education is being shaped by immersive learning, as new technologies continue to revolutionize how children engage with knowledge. Traditional learning methods are gradually giving way to interactive, experiential, and adaptive learning models, which make education more engaging and effective. The next generation of immersive learning is being driven by advances in artificial intelligence (AI), mixed reality (MR), gamification, and real-world integration, providing children with more personalized and dynamic educational experiences.

### AI-Driven Adaptive Learning for Customized Education

AI-powered platforms like Squirrel AI and DreamBox are transforming education by personalizing learning experiences to suit each child’s strengths and weaknesses. These systems use real-time data and machine learning algorithms to adjust lesson difficulty, provide instant feedback, and identify areas where students need extra support. This ensures that every child progresses at their own pace, making learning more efficient, enjoyable, and tailored to individual needs.

### Mixed Reality (MR): The Future of Interactive Learning

Mixed reality combines the best of virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR), creating a more immersive educational environment. MR-powered learning tools, such as Microsoft HoloLens and Magic Leap, allow children to manipulate virtual objects, explore 3D environments, and engage in hands-on digital experiments. This bridges the gap between physical and digital learning, enabling students to experience concepts in science, engineering, and medicine in ways that were previously impossible.

### Gamification: Make Learning an Adventure

Gamification is making learning more exciting by integrating game mechanics into educational content. Platforms like Prodigy Math, Classcraft, and Duolingo for Kids use points, rewards, and challenges to motivate students to engage with difficult subjects. Research shows that students who learn through gamification demonstrate higher retention rates, increased problem-solving skills, and better critical thinking abilities.

### Real-World Integration: Bringing Education into Everyday Life

Immersive learning is extending beyond the classroom, integrating education into everyday experiences. Augmented reality learning apps, smart assistants, and interactive museum exhibits allow children to engage with educational content seamlessly throughout their daily lives. For example, AR-powered museum tours bring historical events to life, and AI-driven learning companions assist children with homework in real-time. This trend ensures that learning remains continuous, engaging, and contextually relevant.

The Bottom Line
---------------

Immersive learning is revolutionizing the way children learn, making education more interactive, engaging, and tailored to individual needs. With advancements in AI, VR, AR, and gamification, children can now experience subjects in ways that were once unimaginable. Whether they are exploring space in a VR simulation, coding their own robots, or engaging in interactive storytelling through AR, these learning methods foster critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills that prepare them for the future.

As immersive learning continues to evolve, it is expected to become a core component of education systems worldwide, offering children personalized and engaging learning experiences. Parents considering STEM summer camps or educational enrichment programs should look for those integrating immersive technologies, as they provide children with a fun, hands-on, and future-ready learning experience that extends beyond the classroom.

FAQs
----

**What is immersive learning for kids?**

Immersive learning uses virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), gamification, and interactive simulations to create engaging educational experiences that enhance comprehension and retention.

**How does VR and AR help in education?**

They provide hands-on, interactive experiences that improve engagement, enhance memory retention, and make learning more fun by allowing children to explore subjects like history, science, and coding in a fully immersive environment.

**What are the best apps for immersive learning?**

Some of the top-rated immersive learning apps include Google Expeditions, Scratch, Prodigy Math, Kahoot!, Quiver, and ClassVR, which offer unique educational experiences across different subjects.

**Is immersive learning better than traditional methods?**

Studies show that interactive, hands-on learning improves comprehension and retention rates significantly compared to passive learning methods like lectures or textbooks.

**Are there free immersive learning resources available?**

Yes, platforms like Scratch, Chrome Music Lab, Khan Academy, and PhET Interactive Simulations provide free, high-quality immersive learning experiences for students.

**How can parents integrate immersive learning at home?**

Parents can encourage immersive learning by using educational VR and AR apps, interactive STEM kits, and gamified learning platforms that make learning engaging and interactive.

**Do STEM summer camps use immersive learning?**

Many STEM summer camps incorporate immersive learning techniques such as coding workshops, robotics labs, virtual science experiments, and AR storytelling to enhance children’s educational experiences.

**What are the benefits of immersive learning for children with different learning styles?**

Immersive learning caters to visual, auditory, and kinesthetic learners, making it a versatile approach that enhances understanding, creativity, and problem-solving skills for all types of students.

**What careers can immersive learning prepare kids for?**

By engaging with immersive learning, children develop essential skills for careers in artificial intelligence, software engineering, game design, healthcare technology, and environmental science.

**How will immersive learning evolve in the future?**

With advances in AI-driven adaptive learning, mixed reality experiences, and real-world educational integration, immersive learning is expected to become a core part of modern education, making learning more personalized, engaging, and impactful for children.

* [5. Blending Virtual Reality with Traditional Learning](#-Blending-Virtual-Reality-with-Traditional-Learning-)

## How is Virtual Reality Used in Education?

From Europe to America, Asia to Africa, thousands of schools worldwide are delivering [immersive learning](/blog/a-guide-to-immersive-learning-in-schools/) and experiencing the [benefits of VR in education](/blog/benefits-of-virtual-reality-in-education/).

Whether they’re traveling back in time, flying to faraway lands, experiencing the impossible or even creating their own immersive content, educators are creating engaging, unforgettable learning experiences with virtual and [augmented reality](/blog/what-is-augmented-reality-everything-you-need-to-know/).

As you’d expect, there’s a HUGE range of [virtual reality in education articles](/blog/) available to show you exactly how these schools are implementing VR. To save you spending hours searching through hundreds of [virtual reality in education pdfs](https://core.ac.uk/download/pdf/43559881.pdf), case studies and websites, we’ve pulled together the 5 best virtual reality examples in education for you.

So, if you’re ready, let’s get straight into them!

## 5 Inspiring Virtual Reality Examples in Education

### 1. Virtual Field Trips

Field trips are a vital part of a child’s education. By getting out and exploring topics first-hand, educators can reinforce classroom learning, improve knowledge retention, encourage curiosity and boost student engagement. However, in-person field trips just aren't always possible. With Covid-19 restricting access over the past few years, schools worldwide have been unable to venture out of the classroom – limiting students’ opportunities to experience the world. But it’s not all over...

Using VR for virtual field trips, [students can travel to different cities, countries and cultures, all without even needing to leave the classroom!](/virtual-field-trips/) Virtual field trips open up unlimited possibilities, giving students the ability to go anywhere and see anything. From legendary landmarks like Stonehenge and the Eiffel Tower, magnificent museums such as the Guggenheim and the Louvre, and even exciting expeditions to destinations like the Sahara and the Arctic, there are literally no limits with virtual field trips.

As well as opening up access, virtual field trips also offer a time and cost-effective option for schools, as students can fly halfway across the world at the click of a button. What’s more, with [immersive virtual reality headsets like ClassVR](/product-overview/), students will feel like they’re actually there – ensuring the virtual field trips are an unforgettable learning experience! A great example of this comes from [Oak Run Middle School in Texas who have been setting off on global explorations with virtual reality](/using-classvr-virtual-field-trips-oak-run-middle-school/), giving many of their students the opportunity to venture out of their home town for the very first time.

### 2. Exploring Conceptual Subjects

Ready for more virtual reality examples in education?

Understanding theory-based conceptual subjects can be extremely challenging for students. With most learning coming from textbooks and diagrams, how can students properly grasp an abstract concept if they can’t even truly visualise it?

However, with virtual and augmented reality at their disposal, teachers can genuinely bring conceptual subjects to life! By allowing students to experience, examine and explore more abstract subjects, they can visualise and engage with the topic in ways that just aren’t possible with traditional learning.

Imagine you’re a student learning about cells, a topic that’s typically difficult for children to grasp. Would you find the concept easier to understand with a) a diagram of an animal cell in a textbook or b) a detailed, interactive 3D model of a cell that you can hold and investigate in the palm of your hands?

We're putting our bets of the fact your chose B! Using immersive 3D models, students can hold a cell, examine it from all angles and investigate its structure. There are some great examples of this in schools, with educators using [3D models of cells](/state-correctional-institution-elements-compounds-student-age-16-17/) and [360-degree photos and videos](/science-investigating-cells-age-14-16/) to help students understand complex Chemistry concepts.

### 3. Developing Computing and Technology Skills

In today’s world, technology is everywhere. That’s why [computing is now an crucial part of a student’s education](https://itstillworks.com/importance-computer-education-students-1837.html). From maximising learning efficiency to preparing for future jobs and careers, there are many reasons why students need to learn these skills at school... and virtual reality can help!

Virtual reality offers a perfect opportunity for students to develop technology skills in an exciting and engaging environment. Students can practice and hone computing skills by building amazing virtual creations, and then experiencing it all in virtual reality.

For instance, with software like [Delightex](https://www.delightex.com/), students can set their imaginations free by creating their own 3D virtual worlds. It’s the perfect introduction to coding! Alongside developing computing and coding abilities, Delightex boosts creativity in the classroom and is a [perfect tool to use alongside VR](/case-studies/winton-woods-city-schools/). Likewise, [ThingLink](https://www.thinglink.com/) lets students create and augment images, videos and 3D tours – giving them the opportunity to [integrate real-life situations into their learning](https://www.thinglink.com/blog/classvr-and-thinglink-case-study/#.YqDGaqjMK3A).

That’s not all... From integration with Paint 3D to Tinkercad, SketchUp to Sketchfab, there are SO many virtual reality examples in education that can [enhance computing skills](/blog/5-tools-to-create-your-own-vr-content/).

### 4. Immersive Group Learning

It may come as a surprise, but virtual reality is actually a great way to practice group learning and teamwork! In schools around the world, there are so many brilliant virtual reality examples of group learning in education. For one, working in pairs with virtual reality headsets can be a great way to improve vocabulary and descriptive writing skills. While one student explores in VR and describes what they can see, the other student can write it down, creating opportunities for discussion. [Beatrix Potter School in London used this lesson format to venture on an under the sea exploration](/case-studies/geography-under-the-sea-student-age-6-7/), with students working in pairs to verbally illustrate marine life before ultimately writing a descriptive paragraph.

### 5. Blending Virtual Reality with Traditional Learning

Last but definitely not least, [blended learning](https://www.fenews.co.uk/exclusive/is-virtual-reality-a-good-fit-for-your-blended-learning-curriculum-2/) is one of our final virtual reality examples in education. VR is an incredible learning tool within smart classrooms that we’re confident is the future of education. However, it’s important to remember that VR shouldn’t ever be used to replace a lesson. It's not an alternative to teaching, it’s a powerful educational tool that teachers can use to *enhance* their pedagogy. Educators can take their teaching to the next level by integrating immersive, educational experiences into lessons.

In Scotland, Mearns Primary School are a great example of how schools can use VR technology alongside traditional learning. After drawing wraparound landscapes in a lesson, their students used 360-degree cameras to capture the creations, annotated them in ThingLink and explored them using VR headsets!  4 September 2024

In the digital age, education has been transformed by innovative technologies like Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR). These tools not only enrich the teaching process, but also provide immersive experiences for students.

**Here, we will explore what augmented reality and virtual reality are, their major differences, practical examples in the academic sector, and their main benefits, as well as some specific applications to successfully implement them in education.**

What is augmented reality?
--------------------------

Generally speaking, augmented reality consists of integrating graphic elements into the real world, improving our perception of the environment. **In education, this is achieved by superimposing digital information onto physical objects, providing an enriched and contextualized experience.**

### Example of augmented reality in the educational sector

**A prime example is the use of applications that, using a device’s camera, display additional information about images or textbooks** (interactive books). You can also go on virtual tours by visiting historical or geographical sites using mobile devices.

What is virtual reality?
------------------------

**The goal of virtual reality is to immerse the user in a fully digital environment, often through devices such as VR headsets or VR goggles.** In education, this makes it possible to recreate virtual situations for hands-on and surprisingly realistic learning.

### Example of virtual reality in the educational sector

In higher education**,**[**virtual reality**](https://dobetter.esade.edu/en/virtual-reality?utm_source=Webutm_medium=utm_campaign=trans-awareness-beyond_seo_contenidos-mix-blogutm_content=transversal-audience_mixutm_term=articulo-blog_beyond)**enables hands-on simulations, like exploring virtual laboratories or attending conferences in virtual environments**, providing unparalleled experiences.

Person with virtual reality goggles in a classroom, interacting in Esade’s metaverse

As can be seen in the video, **Esade’s virtual campus** consists of a main auditorium, where relevant academic sessions are taught and presentations or videos can be viewed; several meeting rooms for discussion and group work; as well as a recreational area, which offers experiences such as basketball or archery. And, of course, there is an area where participants can interact with each other and move from one space to another.

Differences between augmented reality and virtual reality
---------------------------------------------------------

**Augmented reality and virtual reality are fundamentally different in the way they interact with the environment. While augmented reality enhances the real world by overlaying virtual elements, virtual reality creates a completely new environment**, immersing the user in a fully digital experience.

In other words, augmented reality modifies real-world scenes by adding digital information without replacing the surrounding reality; Whereas virtual reality, as its name suggests, creates virtual environments from scratch, disconnecting the user from physical reality and transporting them to a new space.

**Another crucial distinction is that augmented reality uses devices such as _smartphones_ to overlay information on top of the real environment, while virtual reality requires more specialized _hardware_ such as VR headsets or goggles**.

Benefits of augmented and virtual reality in the classroom
----------------------------------------------------------

The implementation of augmented reality and virtual reality in education has obvious benefits. **These technologies not only have a positive impact on academic performance, but also transform the educational experience, making learning more accessible, engaging, and tailored to the individual needs of students.**

*   **More autonomous learning:** AR and VR foster learner independence by enabling interactive and personalized learning experiences.
*   **Improved performance:** the application of augmented reality in education has been shown to contribute to higher academic performance, thanks to the more engaging nature of the content.
*   **Personalization of learning:** virtual reality makes it possible to adapt educational content to individual needs, creating unique experiences for each student.
*   **Increased participation and motivation:** the incorporation of virtual elements attracts students’ attention, increasing their motivation and active engagement in the learning process.
*   **Enhanced learning experience:** both augmented and virtual reality enrich the learning experience by providing interactive and realistic environments.
*   **Facilitates learning:** augmented reality simplifies the understanding of complex concepts by providing visual and practical representations.

Esade student with virtual reality goggles and other accessories at an entrepreneurship fair

Augmented reality applications for the education sector
-------------------------------------------------------

The integration of augmented reality applications in education offers interactive and immersive experiences. The following are some of the most important applications:

### JigSpace

This lets you explore concepts in 3D, facilitating the understanding of complex topics. In addition, **it is a 3D interactive presentation application and platform that allows anyone to create augmented reality demonstrations easily and without programming.**

Some features of JigSpace include the ability to create and share 3D presentations in minutes, instantly view them in augmented reality, and manage a library of 3D content including materials, textures, videos, and sounds.

### Layar

**This is an augmented reality tool that superimposes digital information, such as images, videos, or web links, over the physical environment captured by the device’s camera.**The Layar application has been praised for its usefulness in a variety of contexts, from education to entertainment, and advertising.

### AR Anatomía 4D+

**This augmented reality application provides an immersive and educational way to study human anatomy, allowing users to view and manipulate anatomical models in real time.** By using 4D technology, the application allows for a more dynamic experience, where anatomical models can be explored from different angles and layers.

### Metaverse

**Developed by Metaverse Studio, this free augmented reality Android app is ideal for creating custom AR experiences for the classroom, in record time and with ease.** A variety of tools and templates are used for this purpose. In turn, these experiences can be shared with other users, who can view them using the same application.

>> Did you know that Esade was the first European _business school_ to open a campus in the [metaverse](https://www.esade.edu/beyond/en/what-innovative-business-ideas-does-the-metaverse-offer/)? In fact, since November 2022, Esade offers a virtual space in which _Executive Education_ students enjoy an immersive training experience, interact with their avatars, explore their possibilities, and experiment by mapping future scenarios in which to test new business models that impact society and the planet.

### Arloopa

**The Arloopa app, available on Google Play, is an excellent platform for creating augmented reality experiences.** It also lets you visualize 3D models integrated in the real world. The tool has been used in various contexts, both at the developer level and among end users. It also offers six different languages, including English, Armenian, Russian, German, Spanish, and Czech.

Educational virtual reality applications
----------------------------------------

Virtual reality has transformed education, offering immersive experiences that enhance learning. Here are some virtual reality applications to consider:

### VirtualSpeech

This is a soft skills training platform that uses virtual reality (VR) and artificial intelligence (AI) feedback to optimize communication skills, such as public speaking, developing sales and persuasion skills, among others.

This virtual reality application, used at Esade, allows users to practice in a safe, risk-free environment, reducing anxiety and increasing confidence. It also provides accurate and personalized _feedback_ in real time to improve communication.

### Bodyswaps

This virtual reality application simulates realistic work scenarios, allowing users to encounter authentic work situations and learn by exchanging roles with different perspectives. In short, Bodyswaps is a useful tool for professional training, as it facilitates the development of soft and technical skills necessary for real work contexts.

### VR Math

**This is a virtual reality application designed to improve the teaching and learning of mathematical concepts in an immersive way.** Through three-dimensional environments and interactive experiences, VR Math allows students to explore and understand mathematical concepts in a more visual and hands-on way.

### Anatomyou

Anatomyou is an educational virtual reality application designed for learning and teaching human anatomy in an immersive way. **With this platform, students will be able to explore the human body in 3D using virtual reality.**This interactive experience is undoubtedly very valuable in the field of healthcare, as it allows for an understanding of anatomy from different perspectives.

### Unimersiv

**Unimersiv is the largest platform for educational experiences in virtual reality. Offering a downloadable application, Unimersiv provides access to a variety of virtual experiences and educational activities covering multiple topics.**

### VR Lessons by ThingLink

**This allows for the creation of interactive and immersive lessons using 360-degree images and videos.**Users can use ThingLink to develop immersive educational content that can be experienced through virtual reality devices. In addition, ThingLink facilitates the creation and sharing of virtual tours, making it a valuable educational tool.

### Mondly VR

**Mondly VR is a language learning platform that uses virtual reality to simulate everyday conversational situations.** The application integrates a world-class _chatbot_ and speech recognition to engage users in an interactive and immersive learning process.

With Mondly VR, students can improve their language skills by interacting with virtual characters, giving them the confidence to deal with real-life situations.

**After taking an in-depth look at the role of augmented reality and virtual reality in education, we can conclude that these emerging technologies are having a true transformative impact on the way we teach and learn.**

It is clear that **both technologies present challenges**, such as the need for adequate technological infrastructure and teacher training, but the potential benefits far outweigh these obstacles.

The flexibility, personalization of learning, and intrinsic motivation offered by augmented reality and virtual reality suggest [**a promising future for education**](https://dobetter.esade.edu/en/innovation-technology/future-education?utm_source=Webutm_medium=utm_campaign=trans-awareness-beyond_seo_contenidos-mix-blogutm_content=transversal-audience_mixutm_term=articulo-blog_beyond). Likewise, **it is imperative to continue researching, innovating, and collaborating in order to take full advantage of their potential**.
